channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: President Trump cleans house at the bipartisan Election Assistance Commission
item.description: With just months until the midterms, President Trump relieved the remaining members of the U.S. Election Assistance Commission, a move condemned by Democrats and voting rights advocates.
item.content:encoded:
With just months until the midterms, President Trump relieved the remaining members of the U.S. Election Assistance Commission, a move condemned by Democrats and voting rights advocates.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Swasey

item.title: In private call, Education Dept. tried, but failed, to reassure disability advocates
item.description: The disability community has long worried about what would happen if special education oversight moved from the Education Department to another agency. Now, those moves are becoming more real.
item.content:encoded:
The disability community has long worried about what would happen if special education oversight moved from the Education Department to another agency. Now, those moves are becoming more real.

(Image credit: Kayla Bartkowski/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: Trump's arch clears another hurdle, setting up a big debate: Do height limits apply?
item.description: The Interior Department is arguing D.C. height limits don't apply to federal projects, bucking a century of precedent. If the panel reviewing Trump's arch agrees, experts say it could change the city.
item.content:encoded:
The Interior Department is arguing D.C. height limits don't apply to federal projects, bucking a century of precedent. If the panel reviewing Trump's arch agrees, experts say it could change the city.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: France downs Morocco 2-0 to advance to the World Cup semifinal
item.description: Morocco was no match for France, which lost 2-0. The French, one of the pre-tournament favorites, move on to the World Cup semifinals against either Spain or Belgium.
item.content:encoded:
Morocco was no match for France, which lost 2-0. The French, one of the pre-tournament favorites, move on to the World Cup semifinals against either Spain or Belgium.

(Image credit: Odd Andersen)
item.dc:creator: Russell Lewis

item.title: Smithsonian chief emphasizes 'accuracy and integrity' after White House report
item.description: The memo from the Smithsonian's secretary, Lonnie Bunch, responded to a White House report that calls the National Museum of American History driven by "a radical, activist ideology."
item.content:encoded:
The memo from the Smithsonian's secretary, Lonnie Bunch, responded to a White House report that calls the National Museum of American History driven by "a radical, activist ideology."

(Image credit: J. Scott Applewhite)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: How to deal with seesawing gas prices
item.description: Gas prices have fluctuated since the U.S. and Israel launched a war on Iran, which disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and left consumers unsure of what they'll pay at the pump.
item.content:encoded:
Gas prices have fluctuated since the U.S. and Israel launched a war on Iran, which disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and left consumers unsure of what they'll pay at the pump.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: EPA proposes weakening heavy-duty truck pollution rules
item.description: The Trump EPA calls Biden-era rules for cutting pollution from heavy trucks "unworkable." The proposed changes have been celebrated by trucking groups and denounced by environmental groups.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump EPA calls Biden-era rules for cutting pollution from heavy trucks "unworkable." The proposed changes have been celebrated by trucking groups and denounced by environmental groups.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: A new kind of robot swims the seas and soars the skies
item.description: Inspired by diving birds, roboticists built the lightweight machines to move from water to air. The design may one day lead to robots that can monitor and sample the coastal ocean.
item.content:encoded:
Inspired by diving birds, roboticists built the lightweight machines to move from water to air. The design may one day lead to robots that can monitor and sample the coastal ocean.

(Image credit: Raphael Zufferey)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Vermont is creating a statewide trail, and making sure it's accessible to all
item.description: Mountain bike enthusiasts have been working for years on an ambitious 485-mile, multi-use trail called The Velomont that will span the length of the state. They're making sure it's friendly for people with disabilities, particularly cyclists.
item.content:encoded:
Mountain bike enthusiasts have been working for years on an ambitious 485-mile, multi-use trail called The Velomont that will span the length of the state. They're making sure it's friendly for people with disabilities, particularly cyclists.

(Image credit: Zoe McDonald)
item.dc:creator: Nina Keck

item.title: Class action suit against AI makers over deepfake child sexual abuse material expands
item.description: New plaintiffs in a lawsuit against Elon Musk's SpaceXAI and Stability AI say the companies' AI tools were used to make sexually explicit images of them as children.
item.content:encoded:
New plaintiffs in a lawsuit against Elon Musk's SpaceXAI and Stability AI say the companies' AI tools were used to make sexually explicit images of them as children.

(Image credit: Pablo Vera)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan
